contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Automated monthly AMI backups of the two production DB EC2 instances (Nelanco)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/Cypher-Defense/pull/39
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/341
  
  Details:
  Verified that the two Nelanco database boxes — seni_sql (i-08ebe96afbc649a95, Edgar/seni_ror PostgreSQL, external /dev/sdb 250GiB) and krake_data (i-07c76510b231d787f, krake_ror data/DB, external /dev/sdf 100GiB) — each have an external data disk and were only receiving data-volume EBS snapshots (snapshot_databases.py), with no full bootable AMI backup. Added scripts/aws/snapshot_databases_ami.py + .github/workflows/snapshot_databases_ami.yml in Cypher-Defense: create-image --no-reboot for both instances (captures OS root + all attached volumes incl. the external data disk as one consistent bootable DR image; crash-consistent, Postgres recovers via WAL, never interrupts a live DB), tags AMI + snapshots, per-instance retention keep 6 with pruning of older AMIs and their backing snapshots, monthly on the 1st at 04:00 UTC offset from the existing 02:00 EBS snapshot, reusing the CYPHER_DEFENCE_AWS_* secrets already on the repo. ASCII-only description (create-image rejects non-ASCII). Dry-run validated against live AWS; first real run created seni_sql ami-05098dac19769a8ae and krake_data ami-07f1e676f7c7b14aa. CONTEXT_UPDATES note added.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
